translations.source: Records of the Three Kingdoms [三國志]Scroll 39 (Shu 9)
translations.source_text:
重耳亡居外久，卒得反國，天之所開，不可當。

子玉猶在，庸可喜乎！
translations.translation:
Ma Liang (Jichang)
馬良 (季常)
Lived AD 186–222
aSanguozhiBiographyRecords of the Three Kingdoms [三國志]
Scroll 39 (Shu 9)
byZL181
onDecember 14th, 2022

Ma Liang, styledJichang, was fromYicheng[宜城],Xiangyang[襄陽].
He had five brothers, all of whom had talented reputations. Their hometown would say of them, “The Ma clan has fivechangs, butWhite Browsis the greatest.” There were white tufts on the eyebrows of[Ma] Liang, hence he was praised as such.
When theFormer Lordwas governingJing province[荊州], he was recruited as aClerk. Eventually, when theFormer Lordentered Shu,Zhuge Liangfollowed him, while[Ma] Liangremained inJing province[荊州]. He wrote this message to[Zhuge] Liang.
“I heardLuocheng[雒城] has already been captured, which must be the blessings of Heaven. Esteemed elder brother, you have responded to your destiny and aided this generation, and your participation in the undertaking has brought glory to the nation. Auspicious portents have thus manifested themselves.AIn changing usefulness through consideration and discerning value through wisdom, then in selecting talents, one should follow such beauty.1In this manner, you have moderated brightness2and pleased the remote, spreading our virtue afar to Heaven and Earth, and thereby caused the times to be inclined to heeding and caused the world to submit to the moral way. This has made every melody refined and exquisite, and corrected the noises of Zheng [鄭] and Wei [衞],3all of which has benefitted us in our undertaking, with no disruption to order. This is the zenith of music and the tune of [Bo] Ya [伯牙] and [Shi] Kuang [師曠]!4Although I am no Zhong Qi [鍾期],5dare I not beat to therhythm?”•••
From my interpretation of this hard-to-translate Classical Chinese sentence,Ma Liangis saying that how useful and valuable one is determines whether they are given posts, stay in the posts, or removed from the posts.↩
Here, “moderating brightness” [和光] is used here byMa Liangto refer to benevolent governance and being in harmony with the world, not trying to stand out.↩
Here, “noises of Zheng and Wei” [鄭衞之聲] is a metaphor for lascivious music that was not considered cultured. Here,Ma Liangis alluding to it to say that uncultured customs should be corrected.↩
Bo Ya and Shi Kuang were two masters of music in antiquity, with only talented men like them playing exquisite tunes.↩
Zhong Qi was a woodcutter, and he was considered by Bo Ya to be the only one who understood whatever Bo Ya conveyed through his music. They were like soulmates. After Zhong Qi died, Bo Ya stopped playing music out of sadness.↩
Your minister,[Pei] Songzhi, would comment on this. It was probably because[Ma] Liangswore brotherhood with[Zhuge] Liang—or perhaps they had some intimate relation to each other—and[Zhuge] Liangbeing the elder in age that[Ma] Liangcalled[Zhuge] Lianghis esteemed elder brother.
TheFormer Lordenlisted[Ma] Liangto serve asbureaucratunder theGeneral of the Left. Later, he [Ma Liang] was sent toWuas an envoy.[Ma] Liangtold[Zhuge] Liang, “I have now received the command of the nation to bring the two parties into harmony. I would be fortunate if you would introduce6me toGeneral Sun.”[Zhuge] Liangsaid, “Sir, try writing your own letter.”[Ma] Liangthus composed thisdraft:•••
Ma LiangwantsZhuge Liangto write a letter of introduction.↩
“Thelowly lordhas sent thebureaucratMa Liangto visit as an envoy and sustain the alliance, so as to continue the exploits of Kunwu [昆吾] and Shiwei [豕韋].7This man is an educated scholar with a good [reputation] in theJingchu region[荊楚] who, though inadequate in the forte of quick wit, has the beauty of persevering to the end. We hope you will deign to tolerate him so that he may be assuaged in undertaking thecommission.”•••
Kunwu [昆吾] and Shiwei [豕韋] were vassal states during theXia dynastyand their allies. They remained loyal to theXia dynastywhen theShang dynastyrose up in rebellion, and so the two states were destroyed by theShang dynasty.Ma Liangwants bothLiu BeiandSun Quanto support theHan dynastyno matter what.↩
[Sun] Quantreated him with respect.
When theFormer Lordclaimed the imperial title, he appointed[Ma] LiangasPalace Attendant. Eventually when there was an eastern expedition againstWu,[Ma] Liangwas sent toWuling[武陵] to recruit and receive the Barbarians of the Five Streams. All the mighty leaders of the Barbarians of the Five Streams accepted seals and titles. This was all according to what was intended. When theFormer Lordwas crushed atYiling,[Ma] Liangwas killed. TheFormer Lordconferred the son of[Ma] Liang,[Ma] Bing, asCavalry Commandant.
***
The younger brother of[Ma] Liang,[Ma] Su, was styledYouchang. AsClerkofJing province[荊州從事], he followed theFormer Lordinto Shu [蜀]. He would be promoted toPrefectofMianzhu[綿竹令] andPrefectofChengdu[成都令], thenGrand AdministratorofYuexi[越雋太守]. His talent and ability surpassed many others, and he was good at military strategies.Assisting ChancellorZhuge Liangvalued him greatly and treated him differently. As theFormer Lordwas dying, he told[Zhuge] Liang, “Any of whatMa Susays goes beyond his actual [ability]. He must not employed for anything largescale. Beware, sir!”[Zhuge] Liangstill thought this was not the case, and had[Ma] Suserve asMilitary Adjutant. Every time they met each other to discuss, they would do so from day tonight.B
TheAnnals of Xiangyangstates: In the third year of theera [225],[Zhuge] Liangwent on an expedition against theNanzhong region[南中].[Ma] Susaw him off after about tenli.8[Zhuge] Liangsaid, “Even if we have strategized together over this for years, that is all the more why you should offer a good plan now.”[Ma] Sureplied, “TheNanzhong region[南中] relies on its dangerous terrain and distance. They will not submit for a long time. Even if you defeat them this day, they will rebel again the next day. ShouldYour Lordshipconduct a northern expedition with the entire nation now so as to deal with those mightytraitors, then they [the southern lands] would know that the power of the nation would be weak in the inside and hence rebel very quickly. If you exterminate them to the last remnant in order to eliminate any future problems, that would not be the act of a benevolent person, and moreover, that could not be done in a hurry. In the art of using the military, attacking the heart is the superior way while attacking the city is the inferior way; likewise, warfare of the heart is the superior way while warfare of the armies is the inferior way. I hopeYour Lordshipshall win their hearts and nothing else.”[Zhuge] Liangaccepted this plan and forgave Meng Huo so as to win over the southern lands. Thus, until[Zhuge] Liangwas no more, the southern lands would not dare rebelagain.•••
Assuming that oneliwas about 416 meters, tenliwould have been 4.16 kilometers, or 2.58 miles.↩
In the sixth year of theera [228],[Zhuge] Liangmarched an army towardsQi mountain[祁山]. At the time, there were the veteran generalsWei YanandWu Yi. When the debaters were all in discussion, they thought that they would be commissioned to be the vanguard, but[Zhuge] Liangdefied the masses and selected[Ma] Su, having him command a large force and be at the front. He foughtWeigeneralZhang HeatJieting[街亭], but was routed by[Zhang] He. The soldiers scattered and dispersed.[Zhuge] Liangadvanced, but there was nothing he could occupy, so he withdrew the army toHanzhong[漢中].[Ma] Suwas imprisoned, then died.[Zhuge] Liangshed tears for him. When[Ma] Liangdied, he was thirty-six, and when[Ma] Sudied, he wasthirty-nine.C
TheAnnals of Xiangyangstates: Before[Ma] Sumet his end, he wrote this letter to[Zhuge] Liang: “Your Enlightened Lordshipsaw me as a son, and I sawYour Enlightened Lordshipas a father. I hope you have ruminated deeply over the connotation of executing Gun [鯀] to elevateYu9so that our friendship in life will not be marred by this. Although I may die, I would be without regrets in theYellow Soil.10” At that time, a force of one hundred thousand strong shed tears for him.[Zhuge] Liangpersonally offered sacrifices and treated his orphans as usual.11Later, whenJiang Wanwent toHanzhong[漢中], he told[Zhuge] Liang, “In antiquity, when Chu [楚] killed [Cheng] Dechen [成得臣], theCivilDukewas delighted to know about it later on.12The world has not been stabilized yet, and yet we killed a gentleman with wise plans! How is that not regrettable?”[Zhuge] Liangshed tears and said, “The reasonSun Wuwas able to subdue others and obtain victories in the world was because his application of the laws was made clear.13It was because Yang Gan [楊干] brought disorder to the law that Wei Jiang slew his servant.14TheFour Seasare fractured and divided, and armies have just begun clashing. If we abandon the laws now, how can we then suppress thetraitors?”•••
Gun andYuwere father and son. Although Gun was executed for his incompetence in stopping floods,Yusucceeded him and dealt with the floods successfully.Yuwould be the founder of theXia dynasty.↩
That is to say the underworld.↩
Here, “treated his orphans as usual” [待其遺孤若平生] means thatZhuge Liangdid not punish the surviving children ofMa Sufor the sins of their father, but rather, treated them the same way ifMa Suwas still alive.↩
Cheng Dechen, styled Ziyu, was a general of the Chu state during the Spring and Autumn period. When the King of Chu was attacking the State of Song, the Duke Chong’er of Jin aided Song. Cheng Dechen asked the King to attack Jin, but the King said, “Chong’er has fled from his home and remained outside for a very long time. To suddenly oppose his state is an act despised by Heaven. This is not to be done.” [重耳亡居外久，卒得反國，天之所開，不可當。] But Cheng Dechen led a small unit against Jin, and lost. Duke Chong’er had feared Cheng Dechen, even saying, “Ziyu is still alive. How could I be glad?” [子玉猶在，庸可喜乎！] However, when Cheng Dechen was defeated, the King of Chu blamed him for ignoring his words, and Cheng Dechen soon committed suicide. When Duke Chong’er heard this news, he was very glad. After his death, Chu would lose even more battles with a capable leader gone.↩
Outside of his book,Sun Wuis best known for adhering to the law. When the concubines of his sovereign disobeyed him when he gave them command over troops, he executed the leading women, despite the pleas of his sovereign. He made it clear that the laws were not to be trifled with.↩
Yang Gan was the younger brother of the Duke of Jin. He disobeyed the laws and thus, Wei Jiang killed his servant to send a message. The Duke of Jin was angry when his younger brother was shamed, but eventually forgave Wei Jiang. Wei Jiang was progenitor to the Wei family, the same family that would eventually partition Jin with two other leading families.↩
Xi Zuochistates: ThatZhuge Liangwas unable to subjugate a stronger nation—how could that not be expected? The people of Jin [晉] conjectured that [Xun] Linfu [荀林父] would be of assistance in the future, and thus, when the laws were overlooked, he achieved merits;15conversely, theAccomplished[King] of Chu was ignorant that Dechen aided him, and thus, when he killed him, he was defeated even more. At that time,16Shu [蜀] was a remote and narrow area, their talents few to the stronger nations. Yet, they killed an outstanding person and settled for mediocre people for appointment. They made it clear that laws overrode talent. By not learning from the way of three defeats,17would it not be difficult for them to accomplish the undertaking? Moreover, when theFormer Lordwarned of[Ma] Su, “He must not employed for anything largescale,” did this not say that he was not a [real] talent?[Zhuge] Liangreceived this warning and yet was unable to undertake it, which showed how difficult it was to dislodge[Ma] Su. In governing for the realm, he wanted to amass resources for their uses, but he did not judge talent and integrity in giving appointments, nor assigned tasks according to ability. He knew that he [Ma Su] was greatly beyond [his actual ability], yet still violated the warning of his lord, and his judgment missed the mark, and thus killed a beneficial man. It is therefore difficult for him to be appropriate of being among those talked of beingwise.”•••
Xun Linfu was a general of the Jin state. He suffered a large defeat against Chu forces, and was to be executed. However, he was spared after someone said to the Jin lord that it would be better to keep him than to kill him, citing the death of Dechen leading to military losses for Chu. Xun Linfu learned from his mistakes and became a better general.↩
That is to say, at the timeMa Suwas killed.↩
This presumably refers to some of the failed expeditions againstWeithat were related to poor generals.↩
ㅤ
